methods.method_name: Sample Preparation and Neutron Activation Analysis (INAA)
methods.description: Process for preparing marine surface sediment samples and analyzing them using Instrumental Neutron Activation Analysis (INAA) to determine isotopic composition.

steps.description: After field cleaning of impurities, the marine surface sediments were transported to the Egyptian laboratories which took part to this project.
steps.searchable_summary: transport and clean marine sediment samples

steps.description: The samples were air dried, sieved through a 100-mesh sieve, dried again to a constant weight at 104 °C for 24 h and finally cooled in a desiccator.
Aliquots of 0.1 g of each sample and 0.1 g of certified reference materials (CRM) were packed in aluminum cups.
steps.searchable_summary: pack sediment samples and CRM in aluminum cups

steps.description: The prepared samples were subjected to instrumental neutron activation analysis INAA by the sector of neutron activation analysis and applied research.
To get the induced activity, samples as well as corresponding CRM were exposed to thermal and resonance (epithermal) neutron fluxes for 14 days.
After irradiation, samples were stored for 3 to 5 days cooling, and then the gamma spectra were recorded for 30 min and again, after two weeks, this time for 90 min.
steps.searchable_summary: record gamma spectra of irradiated samples

steps.description: A Genie 2000 software was used to analyze the recorded spectra while the energy calibration elemental composition determination was done by using a proprietary software.

methods.description: Process for preparing sediment samples and analyzing them using Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry (ICP-MS) to determine elemental composition.

steps.description: About 0.25 mg of each sample as well as corresponding CRM, were digested in a mixture of hydrofluoric, nitric, and perchloric acids in a teflon bomb.
The mixture was heated using a pre-established program which bring sample to dryness.
After that, the dry residuum was again digested with hydrochloric acid and completed with high purity distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: redigest dry residue with hydrochloric acid

steps.description: Finally, all determinations were performed by a Perkin Elmer Sciex ICP-MS instrument.

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data and identify patterns of metal pollution.

steps.description: Univariate basic statistics and multivariate statistical data analysis such as hierarchical clustering and principal component analysis were used to process experimental data and identify the possible webs of metal pollution.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis using PCA and clustering

steps.description: Various pollution indices were calculated to estimate the ecological situation. For instance; enrichment factor, geoaccumulation factor, pollution load index, modified pollution index, and risk assessment.
